title: Why did human societies get more complex?
description: Test your knowledge of what contributed to the growing complexity of human societies!
topic_path: The Neolithic Revolution and the birth of agriculture
Why did human societies get more complex?

assessment_items.question_content:
**What development was most important in allowing some people to specialize in activities other than food production?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: One of these things helped support a larger population, which in turn led to more people being able to specialize in activities and work roles.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one benefit of living in cities?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Population densities in cities were high.

choices.content: High population densities allowed for specialization and division of labor
Low population densities meant little competition for resources
High population densities made for cleaner living conditions

assessment_items.question_content:
**What was one risk of living in cities?**



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Agriculture usually led to _more_ certainty about food supplies than a hunting and foraging setup did.

choices.content: High population densities made it easier for diseases to spread
Greater vulnerability to attacks from other groups
Less certainty about food supplies than living a hunting and foraging lifestyle

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why did cities lead to the creation of governments?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Cities were not isolating because so many people were concentrated in one place.

choices.content: Cities concentrated people and resources, which created demand for methods of protection and regulation
Cities concentrated people, and large groups of people naturally require leaders
Cities kept people isolated; government helped create a sense of community

assessment_items.question_content:
**Why was the invention of writing in early civilizations significant?**


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Different societies often had different writing systems, so they wouldn't necessarily be able to communicate with each other.

choices.content: It allowed common people to access more information
It allowed any society to communicate with another society
It allowed elites to communicate messages clearly over distances and time
